label: Digital SAT TRA (Transitions) format research — College Board alignment audit
summary: Hard TRA items (94/325) were mis-specified as participial-phrase completion items. College Board, Erica Meltzer, and Master Manual §8 all require transition WORDS/PHRASES in a two-sentence passage with ______ at the start of sentence 2. Easy/medium (231 rows) already match option format; hard needs full option + passage structure rewrite.

source_authority: College Board Digital SAT — Expression of Ideas / Transitions
Erica Meltzer (The Critical Reader) — three categories: continuation, cause-effect, contrast
OnePrep / Bluebook — stem always: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?

passage_structure.sentences: 2–3 sentences (occasionally 4 on real tests; Master Manual caps at 3)
passage_structure.blank_placement: Second sentence MUST begin with blank followed by comma: '. ______ transition, rest of sentence…'
passage_structure.blank_token: ______ (six underscores) or _______ (seven) — consistent with existing easy/medium bank
passage_structure.relationship: Single unambiguous logical link between sentence immediately before blank and sentence containing blank
passage_structure.read_strategy: Read full passage first; identify relationship (contrast / cause-effect / addition / example) BEFORE looking at choices

option_structure.type: Transition word or short phrase ONLY — never a participial clause or sentence continuation
option_structure.format: Capitalized transition + trailing comma when blank is at sentence start (e.g., 'However,', 'Still,', 'Consequently,')
option_structure.word_count: 1–3 words per option (≈8–20 characters); all four options same structural class

distractor_engineering.synonym_trap: Second option from SAME category as correct (e.g., Furthermore + Moreover)
distractor_engineering.category_shift: Transition from WRONG category (e.g., However when passage needs Therefore)

option_structure.stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?

easy.passage: Obvious contrast/cause/addition; common transitions (However, Therefore, Moreover, For example)
easy.options: Four distinct categories OR one synonym trap; student identifies category at a glance
easy.example_relationship: Trial failed → second trial succeeded = contrast (Still/However)

medium.passage: Relationship requires reading both sentences; less common but still standard transitions
medium.options: Synonym trap more likely (Nevertheless vs Nonetheless); category shift distractor plausible
medium.example_relationship: General claim → specific finding = For instance / Specifically

hard.passage: Subtle or inverted relationship; may use less familiar transitions (Still, Accordingly, Admittedly, In contrast)
hard.options: STILL transition tokens — NOT longer phrases. Hard = finer distinction within/between categories, unfamiliar word in correct category (Meltzer: Still as contradictor), or competing transitions of same type on rare outliers
hard.what_hard_is_NOT: Participial openers (Blurring the sharp contrast…), clausal continuations, 10+ word options, or sentence-completion frames

challenged.passage: Longer authentic prose (8–10 sentences) but blank placement rule unchanged
challenged.options: Transition word + second sentence of rationale per mkChallengedOpts pattern — NOT participial phrases

key_points: ~5 transition questions per RW module on digital SAT
Three main categories: continuation, cause-and-effect, contrast
Read passage fully before choices; determine relationship in own words first
Hard items may use unfamiliar transitions (e.g., Still as contradictor) — still single-word category markers
Minutiae like nonetheless vs nevertheless are NOT tested; category membership is
2–4 sentences; blank at beginning of second or third sentence
Four answer choices are real transition words/phrases representing different logical relationships
Example: 'Researchers found… ______ gardens located near…' → A) For instance,
Always tests relationship between sentence BEFORE blank and sentence CONTAINING blank
Four relationships: Addition, Cause-Effect, Contrast, Example/Emphasis
Hard = unfamiliar words (Accordingly, Admittedly) — test by inserting into simple sentence
2–3 sentence passages; must read carefully for inter-sentence logic
Hard trap: picking For example when passage already gave an example — need Similarly
Hard College Board items still use transition words; difficulty is nuanced relationship + precise transition selection
Progressive easy → medium → hard tiers all test transition vocabulary, not clausal completion

mis_specification_chain.issue: Explicitly documents intent: 'TRA hard rows with single-word transitions → long participial/clausal phrases'
HARD_TRA_* pools are participial phrases (Blurring the sharp contrast…, Drawing the logical consequence…) — structurally BOU/FSS-style clause inserts, not TRA transitions
Validation REQUIRES hard options ≥10 words and REJECTS simple transitions — directly contradicts College Board format
Hard TRA uses longPassage (6–8 sentences) + longTraOptions; appends '______ the subsequent section…' at END of truncated passage instead of two-sentence blank-at-start structure
Lines 243–249: difficulty==='hard' routes to longTraOptions + balanceOptionLengths (pads distractors with '. as.' fragments)
Lines 398–408: identical hard path — longTraOptions for all hard/challenged
Codifies wrong spec: 'TRA hard: longTraOptions() participial frames'

section_8_correct: Second sentence begins with blank _______ followed by comma
Distractors: Synonym Trap, Category Shift, Register Clash
master_manual_alignment.section_8_missing: No difficulty matrix for TRA — internal tooling invented 'participial hard' tier not in manual

easy_medium_generator_is_correct.note: Easy/medium path already produces correct transition tokens with named distractor formulas — reuse this for hard with harder transition vocabulary

hard_item_defects: Missing ______ blank entirely — passages are truncated 4-sentence LONG_PASSAGES ending mid-clause
Options are 8–14 word participial phrases, not transition words
balanceOptionLengths() corrupts options (e.g., '…policy implications,. as.' / '…cause and effect,. in ways.')
Logical relationship between sentences not tested — student picks rhetorical meta-phrases
Same LONG_PASSAGE templates recycled; blank-at-end design from forceSubtype() lost during regen
Challenged subset (25) uses correct transition tokens but bloated two-sentence option padding
Passage corruption: orphan fragments ('a.', 'according.') from padToSentenceRange / trimToWordRange
db_audit.easy_medium_status: Option format CORRECT (However, Still, etc. + comma). Passage has proper '. ______' placement. Minor issue: identical boilerplate passage across many rows (regen artifact) — content refresh still needed but format is valid.

good_easy.passage: The first trial failed to detect a statistically reliable effect despite adequate sample size. ______ the second trial, which enrolled more participants and extended the observation window, showed a clear pattern consistent with the researchers' hypothesis.
good_easy.why_good: Two sentences; blank at start of sentence 2 + comma; options are transition tokens from distinct categories

good_hard_college_board_style.passage: For more than two thousand years, Euclid's text was the paradigm of mathematical argumentation and reasoning. ______ by the 20th century, mathematicians were no longer willing to ground mathematics in this intuitive geometric foundation.
good_hard_college_board_style.why_good: Hard = subtle contrast across time periods; options remain short transitions; Nevertheless is less obvious than However but same category

bad_hard_current_bank.passage: Marine biologists studying reef ecosystem 3225 found that parrotfish grazing limits algae growth… At those sites, coral recruitment dropped sharply even when water temperature
options: Blurring the sharp contrast the passage has drawn between the two claims,
Complicating the binary the author has established in the opening paragraphs,
Building directly on the mechanism described above in the preceding analysis,
Adding a parallel example that deepens the same point about methodological limits,
why_bad: Options are participial clause inserts, not transitions
Tests meta-rhetorical vocabulary, not inter-sentence logic
Passage truncated mid-sentence — ungrammatical even with 'correct' option inserted

bad_hard_padding_artifact.option_corrupt: Extending the author's line of reasoning further into the policy implications,. as.
bad_hard_padding_artifact.why_bad: balanceOptionLengths() padDistractorToChars appended fragment after hard trim — produces nonsense punctuation

actions: Deprecate longTraOptions() for TRA — keep function only if repurposed for non-TRA types
Add TRA_HARD_SETS to english-rw-regen.mjs with less common transitions: Admittedly, In contrast, Conversely, Even so, Accordingly, Hence, Likewise, Notably, To illustrate
Remove HARD_TRA_OPTION_MIN_WORDS gate for transition subtype; add TRA_OPTION_MAX_WORDS = 4 and reject participial-pattern options
Update validateDsatRwQuestion: hard TRA must pass SIMPLE_TRANSITIONS or transition-token regex, not fail it
Update docs/english-rw-regeneration-spec.md L76 and skills.md TRA hard notes
Add validateTraPassageStructure(): requires /.\s+______/ and 50–80 words and 2–3 sentences
Use regenerateTra() easy/medium branch for ALL difficulties — pickTraSet() with tier-specific pools
Hard pool: include Still, Admittedly, Accordingly, In contrast, Conversely, Even so, Hence, Likewise, Notably
Rebuild stimulus: two-sentence templates (50–80 words) with '. ______' at sentence 2 start — NOT longPassage truncation
Derive transition kind from passage logic (contrast/cause/addition/example), not from corrupt existing correct option
Challenged (25): transition word options via ensureTwoSentenceOption ONLY on explanations, not participial inserts
Do NOT call balanceOptionLengths on TRA — use padTransition() width padding (≤15% char spread) per existing easy path
Replace identical boilerplate passage (trial failed / second trial) with topic-rotated 2-sentence stimuli
Ensure passage logic matches assigned correct transition category
SQL filter: skill_id='TRA' AND source_metadata->>'rw_subtype'='transition' AND is_platform_question=true
Post-apply gates: 100% blank_after_period; 0% participial options; avg option len ≤20 chars; 0% spread violations

fix_formula.regen_pseudocode: if (difficulty === 'hard') { options = pickTraSet(traKindFromPassage(stimulus), index, TRA_HARD_SETS); } else { options = pickTraSet(..., TRA_SETS); } stimulus = buildTwoSentenceTraPassage(topic, kind, 50, 80); // never longTraOptions, never balanceOptionLengths

spelling_audit_approach.scope: Full English RW bank (4225 questions) — stimulus_text, question_text, options[].text, explanation

phase_1_extract.sql: SELECT id, skill_id, difficulty, stimulus_text, question_text, options, explanation FROM questions WHERE section = 'reading_writing' AND is_platform_question = true

patterns: Truncated words at passage end (no terminal punctuation)
Repeated boilerplate clauses: 'within limits the authors explicitly acknowledge'
phase_2_mechanical_flags.note: TRA hard audit found multiple mechanical corruption patterns from padding — run before spell-check

tool_options: npx cspell lint --words-only (add project wordlist for Tier-2/3 SAT vocab)
hunspell -l via CLI on extracted plain text
phase_3_dictionary_check.wordlist_additions: TIER23_WORDS from english-rw-templates.mjs + domain terms (parrotfish, cortisol, hieroglyphic, etc.)
phase_3_dictionary_check.fields: Tokenize options separately (short transitions may flag — whitelist TRANSITION_LEXICON)

phase_5_apply.method: Batch SQL UPDATE for confirmed typos only; regenerate items with structural corruption (orphan fragments)
phase_5_apply.validation: Re-run mechanical flags — target 0 orphan-fragment matches post-fix

key_findings: College Board TRA items ALWAYS use transition words/phrases (However, Still, Consequently) — never participial clause completions
Master Manual §8 specifies blank at START of sentence 2 + comma; hard tier is NOT defined as longer options
231/325 easy+medium rows already have correct option format; 94 hard rows are structurally wrong (69 participial, 25 challenged with transition words but bad passages)
Root cause: upgrade-english-content.mjs + longTraOptions() + HARD_TRA_OPTION_MIN_WORDS validation enforces the inverse of real SAT format
Hard DB passages have 0% blank markers vs 100% for easy/medium — passages are truncated LONG_PASSAGES, not two-sentence TRA stimuli
Fix: unify all tiers on pickTraSet() transition tokens; rebuild hard passages; remove longTraOptions from TRA pipeline
